description_long: This is a three-story research institute building with a flat, overhanging roof and a modern, minimalist design. The facade features large rectangular windows arranged in a grid pattern, providing ample natural light to the interior spaces. The front of the building includes a prominent accessible ramp leading to the main entrance, flanked by two decorative planters. A fenced garden area with greenery surrounds two sides of the building, adding a touch of nature to the urban setting. The exterior walls are smooth and light-colored, with subtle horizontal and vertical lines accentuating the structure. Balconies and open corridors are visible on the upper floors, suggesting communal or collaborative spaces for research staff and visitors.
description: A modern, three-story research institute with a flat roof, large windows, accessible ramp, and fenced garden areas. The building features balconies and open corridors, emphasizing a functional yet welcoming design.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance with an accessible ramp, large windows, balconies, and a fenced garden area to the left.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the building's solid wall with minimal windows and the continuation of the garden fence.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a mostly blank wall with a few small windows and the garden fence running along the back.
description_view_3: The right side view reveals additional windows, a secondary entrance or exit, and the garden fence extending along the side.
attribute_entryway_design: The main entryway is wide and accessible, featuring a long, straight ramp with side railings leading up to double glass doors. The entrance is flanked by two planters and is sheltered by the building's overhanging roof. The design prioritizes accessibility and simplicity, with no visible steps or ornate decorations.
